data.output:
ID:       ddx-424fdac4
Title:    Ratify DDx specs for Fizeau-owned routing and no-agent cleanup
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:docs, area:spec, area:fizeau, kind:design, breaking, decomposed
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-f088a3fd
Deps:     ddx-e2586179, ddx-242d2781, ddx-55559451, ddx-119ddb10, ddx-b42d73be
Desc:     PROBLEM
Implementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. Today the specs still mix the old `agent` vocabulary with the desired DDx/Fizeau boundary, so agents can reasonably follow stale instructions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 still describes an `Agent Passthrough` structural mount for `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the specs before implementation. Required docs: FEAT-001, FEAT-006 or its renamed replacement, FEAT-010, ADR-024, TP-020, docs/migrations/routing-config.md, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md, and DDx skill references. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.

NON-SCOPE
No production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. No generated CLI docs rebuild unless the docs generator is part of the spec-doc workflow.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz` contract identity
- fizeau-400b57fe for Fizeau-owned model alias/fuzzy matching

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Dependencies: none; this bead comes first.
Accept:   1. FEAT-001 removes the `ddx agent` structural mount and defines only `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and any Fizeau diagnostics namespace that remains, with no aliases.
2. FEAT-006 is renamed or rewritten as the DDx consumer spec for Fizeau, and no longer presents `agentlib.DdxAgent` / `ddx-agent` as the desired contract name.
3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.

ID:       ddx-ddacd4ff
Title:    C14: delete ExecuteBead* shims and aliases; verify all callers ported
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:cleanup
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-387a0178, ddx-3ee4ddcf
Desc:     PROBLEM
After the C13 refactor (ddx-387a0178), shim typedefs of the form ExecuteBeadWorker, ExecuteBeadReport, ExecuteBeadResult, ExecuteBeadStatus, ExecuteBeadOutcome remain in the codebase as temporary aliases. These shims prevent callers from adopting the canonical names and leave dead identifier surface that grep/tooling cannot distinguish from active usage.

ROOT CAUSE
C13 (ddx-387a0178) introduces the canonical type names as part of the file/identifier rename step and adds shim typedefs for backward compat during the migration window. The shim typedefs live in the files renamed by C13 (exact file:line determined after C13 lands). The CLI aliases ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead continue to register live cobra commands after C13, pointing at logic now accessible via ddx work and ddx try.

PROPOSED FIX
- Sweep all callsites off the shim names (grep for ExecuteBeadWorker | ExecuteBeadReport | ExecuteBeadResult | ExecuteBead\b | ExecuteBeadStatus | ExecuteBeadOutcome in cli/).
- Delete the shim typedefs from C13.
- Remove cobra command registrations for ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead; replace with a cobra RunE that prints a deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
- Verify: grep returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test files.

NON-SCOPE
- Deleting the entire ddx agent command group (that is ddx-d8474e0e, a separate bead).
- Changing execute-loop/try behavior.
Accept:   1. grep -rn 'ExecuteBeadWorker\|ExecuteBeadReport\|ExecuteBeadResult\|ExecuteBead\b\|ExecuteBeadStatus\|ExecuteBeadOutcome' cli/ returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test source files.
2. ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead cobra commands replaced with deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
3. TestNoBuildRegressions_AfterShimDelete: cd cli && go build ./... green.
4. cd cli && go test ./... green.
5. Server worker integration test green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
7. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.RecoverOrphans (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:383) is invoked from the ddx try / ddx work code path. Today it is called ONLY from cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:168 across the entire CLI tree (verified by grep). If it is not yet wired into the try/work path, lift the call into ExecuteBeadWithConfig (engine) or into try.Attempt before this bead's cobra-replacement step. A regression test (TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, in cli/cmd/try_test.go) must assert the behavior survives.

data.text: The dependency bead `ddx-ddacd4ff` is open and the prerequisite function lifts are not done. Writing `no_changes_rationale.txt`.

input.content:
status: blocked
reason: AC #12 + AC #13 prerequisites unmet — ddx-ddacd4ff (C14) is open and the required function lifts are not in the codebase.

Verified blockers:

1. ddx-ddacd4ff (C14) status: open
   AC #13 of this bead states: "This bead's full retirement step inherits those lifts as prerequisites."
   ddx-ddacd4ff must be closed before ddx-d8474e0e can proceed.

2. agent.RecoverOrphans NOT lifted into try/work path.
   grep evidence: only call site is cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:168.
   Required destination (try.Attempt or ExecuteBeadWithConfig engine) has zero calls to RecoverOrphans.
   Deleting agent_execute_bead.go before the lift would silently drop orphan recovery.

3. agent.BuildLandingGateContext NOT in try path.
   Called at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:206 but not in cli/cmd/try.go or try.Attempt.
   cli/internal/server/workers.go:1747 does call it, so server path is covered,
   but the cobra try surface does not invoke it.

4. agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService NOT in try path.
   Called at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:161 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1515 (cobra wrappers)
   and cli/internal/server/workers.go:382 (server), but not in cli/cmd/try.go or work.go.

5. AC #10 required tests do not exist:
   - TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn  → missing in cli/cmd/try_test.go
   - TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding            → missing
   - TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead → missing
   - TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop → missing
   - TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2                 → missing
   These tests must be written (by ddx-ddacd4ff or a sibling bead) before the
   agent CLI surface can be retired.

What needs to happen before this bead can execute:
  a. Close ddx-ddacd4ff (C14): lift RecoverOrphans, BuildLandingGateContext,
     and ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService into ExecuteBeadWithConfig or try.Attempt.
  b. Add the five AC #10 tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go and cli/cmd/work_test.go.
  c. Re-queue ddx-d8474e0e after ddx-ddacd4ff is merged.

data.text:
This bead is blocked. The `no_changes_rationale.txt` documents the three concrete blockers:

1. **`ddx-ddacd4ff` (C14) is still open** — AC #13 makes it an explicit prerequisite.
2. **`agent.RecoverOrphans` not lifted** — only call site is `agent_execute_bead.go:168`; deleting that file without the lift would silently drop orphan recovery from the `ddx try` path.
3. **`agent.BuildLandingGateContext` and `agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` not in try/work path** — still only in the cobra wrappers.
4. **Five required AC #10 tests don't exist** (`TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn`, etc.) in `try_test.go`/`work_test.go`.

The `verification_command` is `ddx bead show ddx-ddacd4ff | grep -q 'Status:.*closed'` — the execution framework will re-queue this bead once C14 is closed.
